title: euphorbia helenae subsp. helenae
intro: Euphorbia helenae subsp. helenae, also known as Helen's copper spur, is a succulent plant species native to the Canary Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions, including inflammation, wounds, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Euphorbia helenae subsp. helenae include its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which make it effective in fighting off infections and promoting wound healing. Euphorbia helenae subsp. helenae can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
